# Spec context: §10.6 — Outdated lyrics alignment

## Source

- Upstream: <https://abcnotation.com/wiki/abc:standard:v2.1#outdated_lyrics_alignment>
- Cached at: `crates/kaijutsu-abc/docs/abc-spec-cache.md` (line 2317)

- Subsection: 10.6 Outdated lyrics alignment

under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 — see `tests/fixtures/spec/README.md`.

## Preceding prose

> Abc 2.1 introduced an extension to [lyrics alignment](#alignment "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") meaning that lyric lines (i.e. those using the `w:` field) no longer need to follow immediately after the line of [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") to which they are attached, meaning tha
> *Examples:* The following two excerpts are equivalent in abc 2.1; under abc 2.0 and previous versions of the standard, only the first version would be legal. Note that there are 4 ([numbered](#numbering "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵")) verses and hence 4 `w:` fields for each line of [music code](#music_code_
> In the first excerpt the lyrics follow immediately after the line of [music code](#music_code_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") to which they are attached.
> In the second excerpt, the lyrics are postponed to the end of the tune, arguably aiding readability substantially and meaning that each verse is contiguous. The [comment lines](#comment_definition "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") in the second excerpt (those lines beginning with `%`) are added for readability
